name: Dilis
description: Small, schooling fish found near the coast, often used for fish paste and other local dishes. Also known as Bolinao or Tiki-Tiki.

name: Galunggong
description: A staple fish in Filipino diets, known locally as 'Galunggong,' and a significant catch in many parts of the Philippines, including the Ilocos Region.
location: Coastal waters of the Ilocos Region

name: Skipjack Tuna
description: An important pelagic tuna species commonly caught in the waters off Luzon, including the Ilocos coast.
location: Offshore waters of the Ilocos Region

name: Groupers
description: A group of high-value reef fish prized for their firm, white flesh. Found in coral reefs and rocky areas along the Ilocos coast.
location: Coral reefs and rocky areas off the Ilocos coast

name: Malaga
description: A popular rabbitfish in Ilocano cuisine, often prepared as kilawin (ceviche) or inabraw (fish soup). Common in seagrass beds and reef areas.

name: Maya-Maya
description: Commercially important reef fish with firm, flavorful flesh. Known locally as Maya-Maya and found in reefs off the Ilocos coast.
location: Coral reefs off the Ilocos coast

name: Cavalla
description: Active predatory fish often associated with reefs and coastal areas. Also known as Talakitok.
location: Coastal waters of the Ilocos Region

name: Yellowfin Tuna
description: A migratory pelagic tuna species highly valued for its meat. Also known as Bariles (juvenile).
location: Offshore waters accessible from the Ilocos Region

name: Tilapia
description: A widely farmed freshwater fish known for being a low-cost protein source in local communities.
